title: Jarren Duran had another confrontation with a fan at Fenway Park
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jarren Duran had an altercation with a fan during the Red Sox's 4-1 win over the Yankees at Fenway Park.
A Yankees fan heckled Duran, prompting his response; the fan was later escorted out by security.
Duran has had multiple fan-related incidents, including one involving a middle finger in April 2026.

title: Brookline party store, Chinese restaurant both close after decades in business
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Golden Temple, a Chinese restaurant in Brookline, closed after 66 years in business.
Party Favors, a bakery and party store on Beacon Street, will close on July 15 after 35 years.
Both businesses expressed gratitude to their customers on social media and are offering parting messages online.

title: Celtics reportedly sign Ron Harper Jr. to 3-year deal
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics re-sign Ron Harper Jr. to a three-year, $9 million contract, declining his $2.6 million team option.
Harper, son of NBA champion Ron Harper, played 26 games for Boston, averaging 4.0 points and 1.7 rebounds.
Boston Celtics considering significant roster changes, focusing on decisions about players like Jaylen Brown and others.

title: 2 JP residents bitten by raccoons in separate incidents, animal control says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Two Jamaica Plain residents were bitten by raccoons in separate incidents over the weekend, according to officials.
One attack occurred at Jamaica Pond and the other near Kenton Street; both victims received medical treatment.
Animal control has not located the raccoons and advises caution around wild animals due to rabies risk.

title: Photos: Here are the best scenes from the Norway-France World Cup matchup at ‘Boston Stadium’
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Ousmane Dembélé scored a hat-trick as France defeated Norway 4-1 in a World Cup match at Gillette Stadium.
Over 64,000 spectators attended the final group stage game in Foxborough during the 2026 World Cup.
Kylian Mbappe assisted twice, while Norway's Andreas Schjelderup was a standout player despite the loss.

title: 5 prospects to watch during Bruins Development Camp
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Dean Letourneau and Cooper Simpson are key prospects in Boston Bruins' Development Camp at Warrior Ice Arena, June 29-July 2.
Max Lundgren, a promising goalie from Merrimack, signed by Boston, will be closely observed during the camp in Brighton.
Elliott Groenewold, a Quinnipiac defenseman, noted for strong defensive skills, participates in Bruins' development activities.

title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A duck boat overturned on a ramp at 10 Museum Way in Cambridge, injuring 11 people, one seriously.
The incident occurred as the duck boat, incapacitated on the Charles River, was being towed by another boat.
Boston Duck Tours suspended operations for the day and is investigating the incident with authorities.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins traded two first-round picks to acquire winger JJ Peterka from Utah Mammoth.
JJ Peterka, 24, has averaged 26 goals per season over the last three years with Buffalo and Utah.
Bruins GM Don Sweeney believes Peterka's skills and age fit well with the team's current roster needs.

title: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs next week
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs like Wegovy and Zepbound starting July 1, affecting 22,000 members.
The policy change is part of Gov. Maura Healey’s fiscal 2027 budget to save an estimated $15 million annually.
MassHealth will still cover these medications for approved conditions like Type 2 diabetes, but not for weight loss.

title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins selected seven players in Rounds 2-7 of the 2026 NHL Draft, including two goalies.
Notable picks include Yuri Ivanov, a Russian goalie, and Nils Bartholdsson, a Swedish winger with speed and skill.
The Bruins also picked defensemen Jacob Vandeven and Cullen McCrate to strengthen their blue line prospects.
